programmes.show_name: New: The Rocky Mountain Mortician Murder
programmes.show_description: Burying The Guilty. The murder trial begins, and a new investigator uncovers surprising new leads. A shocking court outcome sparks outrage. S1, Ep3
This Cyst Persists. Half of Mary's body is a size 18 while the other is an 8. Anthony's cyst is in the area called the Triangle of Death, and Shane wants to get a lump off his back. S1, Ep4
A Lipoma Jackpot. Belinda's lump on her neck causes her to be immobile. Nicole won't get married until she gets her arm pit lump removed. And, Nick unwisely removed a lipoma at home. S1, Ep5
Ashley D's Story. Weighing over 700 pounds at 26-years-old, the clock is ticking on Ashley's life. Ashamed of her body, surgery offers hope, but not without setbacks. S4, Ep2
Chad's Story. At 700 pounds, Chad undergoes gastric bypass surgery. He quickly sheds pounds, but faces a medical crisis when he does not follow the doctor's orders. S4, Ep3
I Got Engaged Last Night! Monique has waited 20 years to get a dress that makes her look like a queen. Plus, Lauren wants a dress with wings, and Debbie has her third fitting. S20, Ep5
I'm Gonna Be The Problem Child Here. Bride Nina rushes to find a reception dress she can bust a move in! Couple Ashley and Amber need to find a jumpsuit for Amber and a gown for Ashley. S20, Ep6
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Weekend With Birdie. Mike is excited to bring a new stray dog into the home. But Molly isn't so sure and has trouble warming up to the new family member. S6, Ep7
The Wreck Of The Vincent Moranto. Despite promising to work together, Vince bails on finishing a do-it-yourself project with Mike. S6, Ep8
Baby, Please Don't Go. Molly learns that a former student is not only pregnant but homeless. When she brings her home, family and friends are concerned. S6, Ep9
Dolomite, Apple Slices. Sheldon makes a new friend who introduces him to a new field of science, while his parents disagree on how to handle Sheldon's blossoming social life. S1, Ep15
Killer Asteroids, Oklahoma. A disappointing loss at the science fair sparks an existential crisis and sends Sheldon's life in a new direction. S1, Ep16
Jiu-Jitsu, Bubble Wrap. When Sheldon is bullied by an unlikely character, Mary and George disagree on how to handle the situation. S1, Ep17
The Cornhusker Vortex. Hoping to fit in with Penny's football-loving friends, Leonard gets tutored in the sport by Sheldon. Raj is furious over the loss of his patang. S3, Ep6
The Guitarist Amplification. Leonard and Penny's first big fight sees Sheldon playing mediator due to childhood trauma that means he can't be around loud verbal conflicts. S3, Ep7
The Vengeance Formulation. Barry Kripke pranks Sheldon during his spot on NPR, leaving him wanting vengeance. Bernadette pressures Wolowitz to define their relationship. S3, Ep9
The Gorilla Experiment. Penny seeks Sheldon's help to better comprehend Leonard's physics work. Wolowitz sees green when Leonard invites Bernadette to watch an experiment. S3, Ep10
The Maternal Congruence. A Christmas visit from his mother has Leonard discovering she's kept a lot of news from him that she shares with Sheldon. S3, Ep11
The Psychic Vortex. Leonard is aghast to find out that Penny believes in psychics. Sheldon tries to assist Koothrappali in picking up a girl at a university party. S3, Ep12
The Bozeman Reaction. A robbery of their apartment sees Leonard and Sheldon call on their friends to devise a security system. S3, Ep13
The Einstein Approximation. Stuck on the solution to a physics problem, Sheldon tries different methods to change his thinking, including working at The Cheesecake Factory. S3, Ep14
Baby Bump. Mike and Molly realise that Frannie doesn't have any plans in place for after the baby is born. Molly decides to help by organising a family reunion. S6, Ep10
The Adoption Option. The adoption agency has scheduled a home visit and Mike and Molly must be on their best behaviour to make a good impression. S6, Ep11
Curse Of The Bambino. Carl convinces Mike to go with him to see a psychic. Meanwhile, Peggy drags Molly to her church to pray for something they've been waiting for. S6, Ep12
A Mother, A Child. When Mary bans Sheldon from reading a mature comic book, Sheldon decides it's time to assert his independence from his mother. S1, Ep18
Gluons, Guacamole. When Sheldon is no longer academically challenged at Medford High, he audits a college class taught by a professor. S1, Ep19
A Dog, A Squirrel. A heated argument erupts between the Coopers and their neighbours when a new dog terrorises Sheldon. S1, Ep20
The Large Hadron Collision. When Leonard is offered a trip to Switzerland on Valentine's Day to see a particle accelerator, he must choose between taking Penny or Sheldon. S3, Ep15
The Excelsior Acquisition. An appearance in traffic court comes at the worst possible time for Sheldon, the same day Stan Lee is appearing at the comic book store. S3, Ep16
The Precious Fragmentation. The guys go to a garage sale and find a ring from the Lord of the Rings films, and the rare find threatens their friendship. S3, Ep17
The Pants Alternative. Sheldon's fear of speaking in front of large groups threatens his ability to accept an award, so the gang tries to help him prepare for the big night. S3, Ep18
The Wheaton Recurrence. Sheldon squares off at the bowling alley against nemesis Wil Wheaton. Leonard's miffed over Penny's response when he says, 'I love you.' S3, Ep19
The Spaghetti Catalysis. Following Leonard and Penny's break up, Sheldon tries to hide from Leonard and the gang that he had a friendly spaghetti dinner at Penny's. S3, Ep20
The Plimpton Stimulation. Sheldon hosts a visiting female physicist, but her intelligence seems matched by her libido. S3, Ep21
The Staircase Implementation. A squabble over the thermostat setting leads to Leonard going over to Penny's and recounting the story of how he met and moved in with Sheldon. S3, Ep22
Dara Ó Briain hosts more laughter filled game play with guests Rhys James, Angela Barnes, Sarah Keyworth, Glenn Moore, Milton Jones and Ahir Shah. S1, Ep6
Soon-to-be-married Peter's lack of male friends prompts him to undertake a quest to find true 'bromance' so he can have a best man at the wedding.
The Curious Case Of The Pooting Cyst. Diane has thousands of tumours all over her body that have ruined her life. Plus, Will is worried his shoulder lump will be ripped open at work. S1, Ep6
An American Tail. The large bump at the base of Taylor's spine is more dangerous than she realizes. John has an embarrassing hip lump, and Noelle wants her stretched ears repaired. S1, Ep7
Brittani's Story. Brittani married a man who loves large women but at 600 lbs she can barely contribute to their marriage. After surgery, she must forge a new life. S4, Ep4
June's Story. 600-pound June copes with the tragic death of her son by eating. After gastric bypass surgery, she must confront the emotional issues driving her food compulsion. S4, Ep5
Three B's: Back, Biceps, Booty. Victoria wants to show off her back, curves, and biceps, but Grandma wants her to cover up! Plus, Dominique has a little panic in the stockroom. S20, Ep7
Give The Man What He Wants. Amanda's back at Kleinfeld to find a second dress after her fiancé hated her first pick. Plus, Kindra and Jamel forget a key element of their wedding! S20, Ep8
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.

I See Love. Mike and Molly gather with friends and family for a momentous occasion. They celebrate and reminisce about everything that brought them together. S6, Ep13
Pilot. Teacher Molly hears cop Mike's share at an Overeaters Anonymous meeting. Instantly smitten, she invites him to come speak to her fourth-grade class. S1, Ep1
First Date. When Molly gets a cold before her first date with Mike, she takes some cold medication to help her feel better - with disastrous results. S1, Ep2
Summer Sausage, Pocket Poncho. While Sheldon is obsesses over Meemaw's budding relationship with his mentor, Dr. Sturgis, George and Mary attempt to bond with their other kids. S1, Ep21
Ice Cream, Gentlemen Callers. When Meemaw is pursued by two gentlemen callers, Sheldon interferes, forcing her to set some ground rules. S1, Ep22
A High-Pitched Buzz. Sheldon dismantles the refrigerator to stop an annoying hum and then must get a paper route to pay for the repairs. S2, Ep1
The Lunar Excitation. Howard and Raj set Sheldon up on an Internet dating site. Penny discovers that she can't go back to dating dumb jocks after dating Leonard. S3, Ep23
The Robotic Manipulation. Howard uses a robotic arm for his own pleasure. Sheldon goes on a date with Amy Farrah Fowler, and Penny finds herself accompanying them. S4, Ep1
Cruciferous Veg Amplification. Sheldon calculates that he'll be dead before technology exists to download his consciousness into a robot, so he goes on a healthy-living binge. S4, Ep2
The Zazzy Substitution. Sheldon breaks up with Amy Farrah Fowler, and the guys worry when he starts replacing her with cats. S4, Ep3
The Hot Troll Deviation. Wolowitz is embarrassed when a secret of his is revealed, and Sheldon and Raj battle at work. S4, Ep4
The Desperation Emanation. Sheldon meets Amy's mum and comes to the realisation he no longer wants to see Amy, and Leonard realises everyone is in a relationship except for him. S4, Ep5
The Irish Pub Formulation. When Raj's sister has a one-day layover, he forbids the others from hooking up with her, unaware she and Leonard already did long ago. S4, Ep6
The Apology Insufficiency. The rest of the gang is interviewed by the FBI for Wolowitz's security clearance, with Sheldon accidentally letting slip some damaging information. S4, Ep7

programmes.show_name: New: Dr Pimple Popper: Breaking Out
programmes.show_description: Lessons In Pimple Popping. Dr. Lee meets Dot, who has a large lump growing on her inner thigh. Plus, a new doctor helps 68-year-old Tim with his severe rhinophyma. S1, Ep1

programmes.show_name: New: Dr Pimple Popper: Breaking Out
programmes.show_description: A Juicy Secret. Katrina suffers from oozing, smelly boils that have appeared all over her body. Plus, a patient with Neurofibromatosis seeks help from Dr. Lee. S1, Ep2
